## [00:00 - 03:02]

It's costing them the results. They are too far away from all the goodness that you've experienced, that you're paying clients and students have experienced. And you've got to widen that gap right now. That's what these showcase emails do. So let's talk about a few different ways that you can show off, brag, do a little flex, and help those on In Decision Island realize they've got to move fast. First is a before and after. So these emails, you're going to use words. You can also use photos. And this is where you're going to display what life was like before working with you and what life is like after. So you can show your before and after, but you can also show before and afters of the people that you've helped. These are going to be a little bit more powerful in this category because it shows that your success goes beyond you. That you're not just a one in a million, right? But that you not only got those results for yourself, but you're now able to be such a great teacher, you can take your own results and help teach others so they can have those results as well. So before and afters are super easy ways to showcase through email the work that you do and the results that you get. Next up are behind the scenes. Now so many people can open a magazine. They can look at TV and they can see the finished product, right? They can see what it looks like when it's all done, when it's all pretty and finished. But what they don't get to see are the behind the scenes. What they don't get to see is how much work, how long, what the process was to get to that amazing ending. And that's where a behind the scenes kind of email comes in. So behind the scenes email is going to show all the pieces and parts. ## [03:02 - 06:06]

So behind the scenes, how does it showcase? Well, it shows the work. It shows that this isn't easy. It shows that there's a whole process to the change that you promise. So we've got our before and afters and our behind the scenes. Next are testimonies. So testimonies don't have to feel like testimonies. Instead, they can tell a story of one of your students through quotes. So you can have little pieces and parts of things your students have told you over the years. They can be screenshots and snapshots of things your students are saying inside your communities or after one of your live classes or coaching sessions. Testimonies are so powerful because it's not you talking. So in this moment, you get to be quiet and you let your client, your student take the stage and do the talking for you. And people love hearing from people who they can relate to. And they can relate to your students and clients who've already had success. Next, you can show those testimonies or changes or even meet someone right in the middle of their process with interviews. So in an interview style email, you're going to post questions and then you'll have one of your clients or students answer that inside the email. So the email is going to look way different than most of your typical emails because you'll have like a quote from you and then a quote from your student, another quote from you and a quote from your student. Now you could also set these up as polls. You could say, I asked 10 of my students these three questions and 90% of them answered this. It doesn't have to be exact conversational, but you want to show that you're in conversation, that you're asking questions with the people that you work with and then you're giving little snippets, little quotes from those interviews inside your emails. Next up, you could have an email that is simply full of photos. You can have an email full of displays and images that really showcase your work. These could be showcases of your own work for yourself, things you've built, things you've created. But again, it's going to be even more powerful if these are photos of things that your clients have created, successes that they've enjoyed. Anytime you bring images into an email, it makes it so much more powerful. We are an image-driven society. We are so accustomed to getting to see everything in living color. We do still want email to be primarily words, but bringing in photos for these showcases are incredibly powerful. Next up, you can do heroes. So these are moments where you again, let those who've worked with you, let those clients take the stage and you present them as a hero. ## [06:06 - 09:08]

And we showcase them as, look at this superhero. They went from this to this. They did this whole thing. We share that and we promote them as a hero. That is an incredible way to create a showcase email. Finally, we can also share unexpected benefits. So your people are going to start to get the gist. They're going to start to expect, oh, okay, when I work with her, this kind of thing happens. When I go through this training, then I could expect this result. But what they don't expect is that a lot of other things will happen in their life because of this one thing you taught them. That it's not just one D change, right? This is a 2D, 3D, is there 4D? This is a really powerful kind of change. And the way you can show this is by showing other things that happen if and when they start to work with you. So an example I can share from the gardening world is that I help people set up and plant and grow their own garden. Now people are simply thinking of just that end result, right?
